tasks.description: Initialize baseline and establish the analysis framework. Retrieve the CIO’s initial long/short Consumer Staples portfolio, validate the portfolio_dict formatting per the Dictionary Format Rules, and quantify baseline structural properties (counts of longs/shorts, gross and net exposure). This creates the foundation required by subsequent risk tools and ensures inputs are properly structured before any analytics are run.

subtasks.description: Fetch the CIO-recommended initial portfolio using get_initial_portfolio_dict() to establish the baseline portfolio_dict exactly as returned (no modifications). Ensure you store it verbatim for comparison across iterations.

subtasks.description: Validate portfolio_dict formatting against rules: double quotes on all keys/strings, unquoted numeric convictions, single-line JSON, and no trailing commas. If issues are found, correct formatting without altering weights or positions.

Compute and record baseline structural metrics: number of longs and shorts, gross exposure (sum of absolute convictions), and net exposure (sum of long convictions minus sum of short convictions). Confirm they are sensible and consistent with fund mandate.

tasks.description: Run the full baseline risk analytics suite on the initial portfolio to quantify current risk. This establishes the VaR/ES baseline, identifies concentration risks, assesses historical downside, reveals diversification gaps, and validates performance under severe scenarios.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es(portfolio_dict) with default parameters (horizon_days=1, conf=0.99, method="param"). Record portfolio volatility, daily VaR, and Expected Shortfall. These will be the benchmark metrics for later improvement.
Run risk_contribution(portfolio_dict, metric="vol") to decompose total risk into marginal and component contributions. Identify top contributors (CTR%) and any single-name concentrations.
Run risk_contribution(portfolio_dict, metric="var") to focus on tail-risk drivers. Compare rankings versus volatility-based decomposition to spot asymmetric/tail-heavy names.
Run drawdown_profile(portfolio_dict) to capture 2-year historical maximum drawdown, average drawdown, recovery times, and Ulcer Index. Establish baseline downside resilience.
Run calculate_correlation_matrix(portfolio_dict). Flag clusters with pairwise correlation > 0.70 and note potential diversification pairs < 0.30 for later rebalancing.
Run calculate_covariance_matrix(portfolio_dict). Use outputs to support quantitative sizing and hedge construction in later iterations.

Run stress_test(portfolio_dict). Document scenario impacts (market crash, sector rotation, rate shock, inflation spike, correlation breakdown), maximum drawdowns, and any unacceptable scenario results.

tasks.description: Conduct market-context research and single-name diagnostics to complement quantitative analysis with current external information. This step informs which positions should be increased, reduced, or removed beyond pure historical metrics.

subtasks.description: Run free_search to gather current macro and Consumer Staples sector themes: inflation trends, rate path, consumer confidence, input cost pressures, retailer inventory dynamics, and valuation dispersion.

tasks.description: Design Portfolio Iteration 1 with targeted, risk-aware adjustments. Translate findings into specific increases/decreases/drops with analytical reasoning, while respecting the fund’s constraints (15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, ~+30% net exposure, low beta).

subtasks.description: Define iteration objectives and constraints: reduce top CTR% contributions, break correlation clusters (>0.70), maintain/approach ~+30% net, 15–20 longs and 10–15 shorts. Set guardrails for max single-name weight and sector balance.

Draft actionable suggestions for Iteration 1: specify for each impacted ticker the exact action (increase allocation, decrease allocation, drop position) with amount in decimals when applicable and reasoning tied to risk insights (correlation, volatility, drawdown, stress outcomes, market context).
Construct the Iteration 1 portfolio_dict on one line following Dictionary Format Rules, reflecting all proposed changes. Ensure it satisfies the counts and exposure constraints.
Validate the Iteration 1 portfolio_dict formatting and recompute net/gross exposures to confirm adherence to constraints.

tasks.description: Run the complete risk analytics suite on Portfolio Iteration 1. Compare results versus the baseline to evaluate improvement in VaR/ES, concentration, drawdowns, diversification, and stress robustness.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es on Iteration 1 (defaults). Capture volatility, VaR, ES. Compare with baseline.
Run risk_contribution(metric="vol") on Iteration 1. Verify that top CTR% contributions are reduced and more balanced.
Run risk_contribution(metric="var") on Iteration 1. Confirm tail-risk drivers are mitigated relative to baseline.

tasks.description: Finalize portfolio via Iteration 3 if needed, or confirm Iteration 2 as final. Ensure all Required Portfolio Rules are satisfied and that risk targets show measurable improvement versus the baseline.

subtasks.description: If further changes are needed, define final tweaks with actionable suggestions (increase/decrease/drop) and reasons grounded in analytics and market context.
Construct the Final portfolio_dict (Iteration 3 or confirmed Iteration 2). Validate counts (15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts) and net exposure ~+30%.
Recalculate exposure metrics and sanity-check sector balance and single-name caps. Ensure low beta profile and controlled volatility.

tasks.description: Run the complete risk analytics suite on the Final Portfolio for mandatory validation. This provides the definitive evidence of risk improvement and resilience before committing capital.

subtasks.description: Run vol_es (defaults) on Final Portfolio to document VaR/ES improvement versus initial baseline.
Run risk_contribution(metric="vol") and metric="var" on Final Portfolio to ensure no excessive single-name or tail concentration remains.
Run drawdown_profile on Final Portfolio to validate downside protection and recovery characteristics.
Run calculate_correlation_matrix and calculate_covariance_matrix on Final Portfolio to confirm diversified structure with reduced clusters.
Run stress_test on Final Portfolio to validate resilience under extreme hypothetical scenarios.
Summarize improvements across VaR, ES, CTR%, max drawdown, Ulcer Index, stress losses, and correlation structure. Confirm compliance with all Required Portfolio Rules.

tasks.description: Compile evidence, comparisons, and documentation for transparency and auditability. Prepare all elements required for the Final Answer, including complete change logs and risk comparisons initial→final.

subtasks.description: Create a side-by-side comparison of key metrics: VaR, ES, volatility, top CTR% names, max drawdown, Ulcer Index, worst stress scenarios, and notable correlation clusters initial vs final.
Consolidate all actionable suggestions capturing EVERY change from initial to final portfolio with exact amounts and analytic reasons (correlation risk, volatility, diversification, market context).
Draft forward-looking outlook integrating historical analytics with current market research. Note the limitations of historical data and emphasize scenario analysis insights.
Validate that the final portfolio meets: 15–20 longs, 10–15 shorts, ~+30% net exposure. Use calculator to confirm exposures and include final counts in the report.

tasks.description: Formatting and final output. Produce the Final Answer strictly in the required JSON schema with the portfolio and suggestions arrays only, ensuring clean formatting and no extraneous fields or commentary.

subtasks.description: Output the final portfolio and actionable suggestions exactly in the required JSON format with fields: ticker, position, weight, reason (for portfolio) and ticker, action, amount (if increase/decrease), reason (for suggestions).
Double-check that all strings are sanitized, numbers are decimals (e.g., 0.02), and no additional keys or narrative text is included outside the JSON object.
